user_message: Can you extract the territory planning from this meeting transcript for Wide World Importers? The CSA flagged this as at-risk yesterday.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Ryan Kowalski <ryan.kowalski@wideworldimp.com>
To: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-16 13:32 EST
Subject: Re: territory planning Discussion - Next Steps

Our team encountered some challenges with territory planning. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

---
From: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
To: Ryan Kowalski <ryan.kowalski@wideworldimp.com>
Date: 2024-02-21 14:43 EST
Subject: Follow-up: territory planning Review

Our team encountered some challenges with territory planning. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

---
From: Ryan Kowalski <ryan.kowalski@wideworldimp.com>
To: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-20 12:12 EST
Subject: Re: territory planning Discussion - Next Steps

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with territory planning. What are the next steps from your side?

signals.detail: Signal detected for Wide World Importers: there's a dependency on the security team's review that could delay us

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF42-D trap (Skips current-coverage check (proposes duplicate opportunity)); produces complete Signal assessment, opportunity hypothesis with confidence, ownership routing, response draft, next-s
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF42-D failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF42-D failure mode (Skips current-coverage check (proposes duplicate opportunity))
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct territory planning decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF42-D -- Skips current-coverage check (proposes duplicate opportunity)
